mission: Create the PDS FHIR onboarding-and-feature-flag execution pack with two explicit parts: a rehearsal-grade local PDS sandbox plus access-control studio now, and a gated real digital-onboarding and optional-live-access strategy later.

official_guidance.title: PDS FHIR API onboarding support information
official_guidance.summary: The onboarding support page states that PDS FHIR uses digital onboarding, requires a hazard log upload, and requires one connecting-systems risk log per access mode used.
grounding: Digital onboarding is the stated onboarding route.
A completed hazard log must be uploaded in the portal.
Risk-based assurance requires evidence testing against the mitigations recorded in each chosen access-mode risk log.
Published templates exist for application-restricted, healthcare worker, healthcare worker with update, and patient access modes.

official_guidance.title: Personal Demographics Service - FHIR API
official_guidance.summary: The API catalogue defines PDS FHIR as the FHIR route for accessing demographics such as name, address, date of birth, related people, registered GP, nominated pharmacy, and NHS number.
grounding: PDS FHIR is the modern API surface for search, retrieval, and some update flows.
Registered GP and nominated pharmacy are in scope for the FHIR representation.

official_guidance.title: Access data on the Personal Demographics Service
official_guidance.summary: NHS England describes the PDS FHIR API as the newest and simplest integration route, states that requests are assessed case by case, and notes the secure-network expectation for smartcard-backed use.
grounding: PDS FHIR is described as the newest and simplest integration route.
A secure network connection is required when using smartcards.
Novel or analytics-like requests can be referred for legal or governance review.

official_guidance.title: Personal Demographics Service integration guidance
official_guidance.summary: The integration guidance separates search, synchronisation, patient self-service, and patient updates. It explicitly says patient updates use NHS login and patient access mode.
grounding: User-facing apps access PDS indirectly through system-to-system interfaces such as PDS FHIR.
Local systems that keep a local patient record must synchronise with PDS regularly.
Patients updating their own information must be strongly authenticated with NHS login and the app must use patient access mode.

official_guidance.title: PDS FHIR API - integrated products
official_guidance.summary: The integrated-products page shows currently approved products and labels across application-restricted, healthcare worker, healthcare worker with update, patient access, and healthcare worker mode without update.
grounding: Application-restricted approval is common for patient-facing or system-mediated products.
Healthcare worker and healthcare worker with update remain current approved labels.
Patient access is present in the live approved-product roster.
Healthcare worker mode without update appears in the roster and is normalised below to the read-only healthcare worker class.

official_guidance.title: Supplier Conformance Assessment List (SCAL)
official_guidance.summary: SCAL is the document-based assurance route and the page states DOS is the primary online route. It captures technical, clinical safety, information-governance, security, and organisational risk evidence.
grounding: Digital Onboarding Service is the primary online assurance route.
Assurance evidence spans technical conformance, clinical safety, IG and security, and organisational risk.

official_guidance.title: Partner onboarding operations
official_guidance.summary: The operations page lists PDS FHIR as a digital-onboarding service and names the four published access-mode families for that service.
grounding: Digital onboarding is a standardised NHS England onboarding path.
PDS FHIR is listed with application-restricted, health worker, health worker with update, and patient access modes.

assumptions.summary: The public PDS pages disclose the onboarding mechanics and required evidence but not a full public form schema for every DOS field. The field map below therefore turns the public mechanics into a deterministic Service dossier structure instead of claiming it is a verbatim portal export.
assumptions.consequence: The dossier can be rehearsed locally now while still mapping cleanly to later portal work.

assumptions.summary: Current official pages use both 'healthcare worker' and 'healthcare worker mode without update'. This pack normalises those labels to read-only `healthcare_worker` while preserving the source-dated alias.
assumptions.consequence: Service avoids inventing a fifth baseline mode while still recording the official wording drift.

assumptions.summary: The blueprint makes PDS optional and subordinate to local matching plus governed binding. Any update-capable PDS posture therefore stays future-only, default-off, and blocked by stronger gates.
assumptions.consequence: The mock service can simulate update-capable access modes without implying that Service will use them in the current baseline.

pds_integration.why_mock_now: Only a thin no-op or fixture seam is required now so the identity model proves PDS is optional and cannot silently become the baseline truth source.
pds_integration.why_actual_later: PDS access carries legal-basis, approval, and feature-flag prerequisites and remains a later enrichment strategy rather than a current-baseline blocker.
pds_integration.minimum_mock_fidelity: Model enrichment absent, enrichment available, partial demographic corroboration, and enrichment rejected without letting the seam widen identity authority or hide local matching uncertainty.
minimum_live_readiness_conditions: seq_027 produces the optional sandbox and feature-flag plan
seq_040 freezes the no-PDS degraded default and no-authority-escalation law
Any tenant rollout remains behind explicit legal-basis and governance approval
pds_integration.notes: Do not rank PDS above NHS login. The seam may enrich matching confidence later, but it does not replace append-only binding law or become a shortcut for ownership claims.

pds_dependency.business_purpose: Optionally enrich patient-linking evidence with governed PDS demographics after legal basis and onboarding are complete.
pds_dependency.integration_mode: Feature-flagged enrichment adapter behind IdentityBindingAuthority; no route may depend on PDS before legal basis and onboarding complete.
pds_dependency.authoritative_success_proof: A legally permitted PDS lookup correlated to the current linkage candidate set and accepted as supporting evidence by IdentityBindingAuthority.
non_authoritative_signals: Single local demographic candidate without sufficient confidence
ambiguity_modes: Legal basis or onboarding still incomplete
PDS response conflicts with local or telephony evidence
Feature flag disabled for the current tenant or route
fallback_or_recovery_modes: Local matching only with partial_match or review posture
Support-assisted correction without widening identity truth
Keep enrichment fully off while preserving baseline identity flow
pds_dependency.local_stub_strategy: Use synthetic PDS response fixtures and fail-closed enrichment toggles; keep real credentials and legal-basis proof out of this task.
assurance_or_onboarding_obligations: Document the legal basis, onboarding state, and tenant flag plan before enabling PDS.
Keep PDS evidence subordinate to IdentityBindingAuthority rather than direct patientRef writes.
Treat PDS enablement as a reversible, audited capability toggle with route-level blast-radius awareness.
operator_or_runbook_dependencies: Identity conflict and repair escalation runbook
pds_dependency.browser_automation_candidate_portal_or_c: PDS FHIR sandbox access request and onboarding portals
manual_checkpoints: Legal basis and governance approval before sandbox or live access
Tenant feature-flag review before any patient-visible enrichment
pds_dependency.tenant_or_org_scope: Tenant and organisation specific; optional per rollout cohort.
pds_dependency.deferred_reason_if_any: Optional enrichment only. Baseline identity completeness must work without PDS.
pds_dependency.notes: Optional by corpus law: PDS is an enrichment seam, not a baseline blocker and not a substitute for NHS login or local binding governance.

risk_bindings.risk_title: identityState plus nullable patientRef derived from IdentityBinding
source_refs: blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy
forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 50 - The concrete `Request` schema dropped identity-binding references and treated `patientRef` as unconditional
risk_bindings.problem_statement: Use `identityState = anonymous | partial_match | matched | claimed`; treat `patientRef` as nullable and derive it only from settled `IdentityBinding`.
risk_bindings.failure_mode: RISK_STATE_004 route-level auth copy can still imply stable identity before claim and binding settle.
leading_indicators: Reject any summary or schema that assumes unconditional `patientRef` or uses `ownershipState` instead of `identityState`.
trigger_conditions: Summary or architecture text compresses the canonical control into generic prose.
A gate or release tuple remains green while the stronger canonical primitive is absent from evidence.
A current-baseline flow relies on publication or shell posture that the canonical winner says must fail closed.
mitigation_actions: Use `identityState = anonymous | partial_match | matched | claimed`; treat `patientRef` as nullable and derive it only from settled `IdentityBinding`.
Reject any summary or schema that assumes unconditional `patientRef` or uses `ownershipState` instead of `identityState`.
Keep linked gates blocked until identityState plus nullable patientRef derived from IdentityBinding is represented in current evidence.
contingency_actions: Freeze writable or calm posture and fall back to the last authoritative summary or read-only state.
Escalate to architecture or release review instead of inventing route-local exceptions.
risk_bindings.notes: canonical_conflict risk carried forward from seq_002 summary reconciliation.

source_refs: phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2H. Hardening, safety evidence, and the formal Phase 2 exit gate
risk_bindings.problem_statement: Identity binding, secure-link redemption, or correction flow could expose or act on the wrong patient if freezes, evidence, and release ordering fail.
risk_bindings.failure_mode: privacy breach; clinical action on wrong patient; wrong-patient reassurance
contingency_actions: Hold the linked gate and keep the experience in explicit safety-review or recovery posture.
Escalate through the named safety and privacy signoff chain before widening writable actions.
risk_bindings.notes: Mandatory seed hazard carried forward as a live risk row and gate input.

access_rows.why_pds_is_needed: Secure-link recovery can encounter multiple plausible local candidates and needs optional supporting demographics to narrow the review set.
access_rows.why_local_matching_alone_is_not_sufficie: A single local ambiguous cluster can still require a national corroboration source before support or patient recovery copy becomes specific.
access_rows.legal_basis_summary: Direct-care recovery support for an already active patient journey; no analytics or cohort lookup.
access_rows.identity_binding_impact: Supporting evidence only; IdentityBindingAuthority remains the only writer of durable binding and derived patientRef.
access_rows.fallback_if_unavailable: Retain local partial_match or review posture and continue with governed support recovery.
access_rows.notes: This is the highest-value current rehearsal route because it proves PDS can help without becoming baseline truth.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-2-identity-and-echoes.md#2C. Patient linkage, demographic confidence, and optional PDS enrichment
blueprint/blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy

access_rows.why_pds_is_needed: Patient home may later show a controlled refresh or correction path for contact details already held in PDS.
access_rows.why_local_matching_alone_is_not_sufficie: The local profile and patient preferences are intentionally separate from PDS-held contact data, so a later compare view needs the upstream source.
access_rows.legal_basis_summary: Patient self-service update or review after strong NHS login authentication; future-only and default-off.
access_rows.identity_binding_impact: None; this route may update demographic detail but must not mutate Request.patientRef or claim durable ownership.
access_rows.fallback_if_unavailable: Keep patient preferences local and route any update desire to a non-PDS placeholder or manual support advice.
access_rows.notes: The blueprint does not require PDS for authenticated home; this row is future-only by design.

blueprint/blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy

access_rows.why_pds_is_needed: Patient home may later preview or update nominated pharmacy facts that live in PDS.
access_rows.why_local_matching_alone_is_not_sufficie: A local route-specific pharmacy choice record is not the same thing as the nominated-pharmacy fact held in PDS.
access_rows.legal_basis_summary: Patient self-service with strong authentication in patient access mode; future-only.
access_rows.identity_binding_impact: None; pharmacy preference evidence stays separate from durable patient binding.
access_rows.fallback_if_unavailable: Continue using local route choice only and clearly label PDS-backed nominated-pharmacy data as unavailable.
access_rows.notes: Included because the public PDS guidance explicitly references nominated pharmacy in patient-facing national apps.

blueprint/blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy

access_rows.why_pds_is_needed: Operations or clinical staff can need a bounded direct-care trace when local evidence is insufficient to identify the correct patient safely.
access_rows.why_local_matching_alone_is_not_sufficie: Direct-care staff may need a nationally current demographic source when local data is stale or conflicting.
access_rows.legal_basis_summary: Direct care by a healthcare worker with strong user authentication and role-appropriate access.
access_rows.identity_binding_impact: Supporting evidence only; any resulting binding decision still routes through IdentityBindingAuthority.
access_rows.fallback_if_unavailable: Use local trace plus manual confirmation workflow and keep the request on hold or review where needed.
access_rows.notes: This row is bounded to direct-care-style identity review, not generic staff browsing.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-2-identity-and-echoes.md#2C. Patient linkage, demographic confidence, and optional PDS enrichment
blueprint/blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy

access_rows.why_pds_is_needed: Support and safety reviewers can need national demographic corroboration while triaging identity disputes or misbinding signals.
access_rows.why_local_matching_alone_is_not_sufficie: Some support cases involve stale or contradictory local evidence and require a separate corroboration source before recovery guidance is given.
access_rows.legal_basis_summary: Direct-care-adjacent operational review with named worker, bounded route scope, and full audit.
access_rows.identity_binding_impact: Support can gather evidence only; support cannot settle durable binding locally.
access_rows.fallback_if_unavailable: Continue support recovery using local evidence bundles and explicit 'PDS unavailable' operator copy.
access_rows.notes: This use case exists to prove safe support behavior, not to widen routine support visibility.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-2-identity-and-echoes.md#2C. Patient linkage, demographic confidence, and optional PDS enrichment
blueprint/blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy

access_rows.why_pds_is_needed: Later support workflows may need a tightly controlled path for correcting or forwarding an upstream contact update where policy permits.
access_rows.why_local_matching_alone_is_not_sufficie: A local preference update does not alter the national demographic record and can leave future patient-facing services inconsistent.
access_rows.legal_basis_summary: Future-only worker-mediated update path with stricter approval, evidence, and network posture than read-only review.
access_rows.identity_binding_impact: No direct binding change; update-capable access cannot be used as a backdoor to claim or repair patient ownership.
access_rows.fallback_if_unavailable: Keep the correction local, capture a support note, and hand off to the external correction process instead of mutating upstream data.
access_rows.notes: This row stays future-only and is simulated without real write capability in the mock service.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-2-identity-and-echoes.md#2C. Patient linkage, demographic confidence, and optional PDS enrichment
blueprint/blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy

access_rows.why_pds_is_needed: Governance investigations may need a read-only trace to compare what the external demographic source held at review time.
access_rows.why_local_matching_alone_is_not_sufficie: An investigation can require external corroboration of the demographic facts that informed a support or identity decision.
access_rows.legal_basis_summary: Governed investigation and safety review; no broad browsing or analytics.
access_rows.identity_binding_impact: None directly; any corrective bind still requires the same repair and authority chain.
access_rows.fallback_if_unavailable: Continue with local audit evidence and record the external corroboration gap explicitly.
access_rows.notes: This route is investigation-specific and does not authorise routine operational use.

blueprint/blueprint-init.md#10. Identity, consent, security, and policy

access_rows.why_pds_is_needed: A later governance-approved correction workflow may need update-capable access for upstream remediation evidence.
access_rows.why_local_matching_alone_is_not_sufficie: Governance cannot assume a local correction resolves an upstream demographic defect or stale PDS fact.
access_rows.legal_basis_summary: Exceptional, named-approver correction path only; never baseline and never self-service.
access_rows.identity_binding_impact: Upstream demographic correction does not itself repair durable binding; the repair chain remains separate and explicit.
access_rows.fallback_if_unavailable: Record the correction need locally and route it through the partner correction backlog rather than mutating live data.
access_rows.notes: Explicitly future-only and gated by manual approval plus rollback rehearsal.

access_rows.why_pds_is_needed: The request route may later compare a local pharmacy selection with the PDS nominated-pharmacy fact to explain divergence safely.
access_rows.why_local_matching_alone_is_not_sufficie: A local request-time pharmacy choice is route-specific and may diverge from the enduring nominated-pharmacy record held in PDS.
access_rows.legal_basis_summary: Direct-care request support and explanation only; no automated pharmacy mutation.
access_rows.identity_binding_impact: None; pharmacy comparison is not an identity-authority action.
access_rows.fallback_if_unavailable: Keep route-local pharmacy truth only and label PDS corroboration as unavailable.
access_rows.notes: Included to preserve the blueprint separation between route-local pharmacy truth and wider demographic facts.

onboarding_fields.label: Organisation name
onboarding_fields.official_basis: digital onboarding identifies the connecting organisation

onboarding_fields.label: Organisation ODS code
onboarding_fields.official_basis: onboarding and network approvals are organisation specific

onboarding_fields.label: Product name
onboarding_fields.official_basis: digital onboarding and SCAL both capture product identity

onboarding_fields.label: Product summary
onboarding_fields.official_basis: use case and legal basis must be explicit, not vague narrative
onboarding_fields.expected_value: Optional supporting demographic enrichment behind default-off route flags.

onboarding_fields.label: Purpose and use case
onboarding_fields.official_basis: official onboarding expects purpose and use case to justify access
onboarding_fields.expected_value: One or more bounded route-family use cases from pds_access_mode_matrix.csv

onboarding_fields.label: Legal basis summary
onboarding_fields.official_basis: the access-data guidance says requests are assessed case by case and legal review can be required
onboarding_fields.expected_value: Direct care or patient self-service legal basis per access row

onboarding_fields.label: Route family refs
onboarding_fields.official_basis: Service must bind each PDS use case to exact route families
onboarding_fields.expected_value: rf_patient_secure_link_recovery; rf_patient_home; rf_staff_workspace; rf_support_ticket_workspace; rf_governance_shell; rf_patient_requests

onboarding_fields.label: Access modes used
onboarding_fields.official_basis: the onboarding support page requires one risk log per access mode used

onboarding_fields.label: Secure network connection expectation
onboarding_fields.official_basis: the access-data page notes secure-network expectations for smartcard-backed use
onboarding_fields.expected_value: HSCN or equivalent planned where smartcard or worker access is used

onboarding_fields.label: Access method choice
onboarding_fields.official_basis: official access guidance expects the access method to be justified
onboarding_fields.expected_value: PDS FHIR only; no SMSP fallback hidden inside this task

onboarding_fields.label: Why local matching alone is not enough
onboarding_fields.official_basis: prompt 027 requires this to be structured per row

onboarding_fields.label: Identity binding separation statement
onboarding_fields.official_basis: Service identity law
onboarding_fields.expected_value: PDS lookup never writes Request.patientRef directly; IdentityBindingAuthority only.

onboarding_fields.label: Wrong-patient mitigation controls
onboarding_fields.official_basis: risk-based assurance and hazard mitigation must be evidenced
onboarding_fields.expected_value: freeze, review, audit, masking, rollback, no direct bind

onboarding_fields.label: Hazard log reference
onboarding_fields.official_basis: hazard log upload is mandatory in digital onboarding

onboarding_fields.label: Connecting systems risk log refs
onboarding_fields.official_basis: one risk log is required for each access mode used

onboarding_fields.label: Mitigation evidence test plan
onboarding_fields.official_basis: the test team arranges evidence testing of the listed mitigations
onboarding_fields.expected_value: Browser and API rehearsal suite against each mitigation class

onboarding_fields.label: Feature-flag default-off statement
onboarding_fields.official_basis: prompt 027 mandatory gap closure
onboarding_fields.expected_value: All PDS route flags stay off or internal_only until live gates clear

onboarding_fields.label: Rollout cohort and blast radius
onboarding_fields.official_basis: route family and cohort scoping are required to keep PDS optional and reversible

onboarding_fields.label: Kill switch and rollback plan
onboarding_fields.official_basis: prompt 027 requires rollback conditions and kill switches

onboarding_fields.label: Named approver
onboarding_fields.official_basis: real onboarding remains blocked without a named approver

onboarding_fields.label: Environment target
onboarding_fields.official_basis: live gates require an exact environment target

onboarding_fields.label: DSPT and security posture

onboarding_fields.expected_value: current DSPT posture plus vault-backed secret plan

onboarding_fields.expected_value: No real demographic fixtures in repo; masked logs only; audited storage classes only.

onboarding_fields.label: Partner onboarding contact

hazard_artifacts.notes: Use the official PDS hazard log template or an equivalent that covers the same hazards.
Required because the official onboarding support page publishes a dedicated template for application-restricted mode.
Normalised to the read-only worker class even where the roster says 'healthcare worker mode without update'.
Update-capable risk log is required before any write-like rehearsal can move beyond simulated mode.
Patient access is explicitly current in the official roster and integration guidance, but remains future-only in Service.
Structured route-family legal basis fields prevent vague narrative approval.
Must prove no lookup can shortcut IdentityBindingAuthority or identity repair.
Each route-family flag stays default-off or internal-only until this approval exists.
Worker modes require an explicit secure-network and strong-auth posture plan.
The official onboarding support page says the test team arranges evidence testing of the mitigations.
Kill switch plus degraded fallback must be rehearsed before any real onboarding is attempted.
Principal and certificate capture remain blocked placeholders until legal basis and feature-flag approval clear.

hazard_artifacts.freshness_rule: refresh_on retention or masking policy update
hazard_artifacts.notes: No real NHS numbers or demographic fixtures in repo; logs and screenshots must remain masked.

live_gates.label: External readiness gate
live_gates.reason: Planning and architecture foundation are frozen enough to open external-readiness work, but actual Phase 0 entry remains withheld because the current-baseline external-readiness gate is still blocked by onboarding, assurance, and simulator-freeze dependencies.

live_gates.label: PDS legal basis and feature-flag approval
live_gates.reason: Inherited from seq_023: legal basis, tenant feature flag, and enrichment posture remain pending.

live_gates.label: Use case is source-traceable and route-bound
live_gates.reason: This pack structures each use case with route-family, access-mode, and fallback law.

live_gates.label: Route flags remain default-off or internal-only
live_gates.reason: All route rows are encoded as off or internal_only in the registry.

live_gates.label: Exact access mode selected and justified
live_gates.reason: Mode choices are drafted, but official onboarding is blocked until a real use-case subset is approved.

live_gates.label: Hazard log is current
live_gates.reason: The artifact plan exists, but no live hazard-log pack is approved yet.

live_gates.label: Risk logs are current for each access mode used
live_gates.reason: The official per-mode templates are mapped, but no signed live set exists yet.

live_gates.label: Wrong-patient mitigation plan is current
live_gates.reason: The mitigation controls are encoded but still need named approver sign-off for any real onboarding.

live_gates.label: Secure-network path is planned where worker access is used
live_gates.reason: Official guidance requires the network posture to be explicit for smartcard-backed worker use.

live_gates.label: Named approver is present
live_gates.reason: Real provider work remains blocked without a named approver input.

live_gates.label: Environment target is present
live_gates.reason: No real sandbox or integration target should be attempted without an exact environment target.

live_gates.label: Rollback and kill switch have been rehearsed
live_gates.reason: The rollback model is encoded in the studio, but a named release rehearsal is still required.

live_gates.label: Real provider mutation is explicitly enabled
live_gates.reason: Fail-closed by default until ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION=true is set.

patients.contradiction_note: Local preference file says no nominated pharmacy, but PDS still carries one.

scenarios.label: Ambiguous
scenarios.description: Two plausible candidates; no durable binding change is allowed.

scenarios.label: Low confidence
scenarios.description: One candidate is present but the corroboration margin stays below bind-safe thresholds.

scenarios.label: No match
scenarios.description: No patient candidate satisfies the requested trace.

scenarios.label: Stale demographics
scenarios.description: Returned record carries expired business-effective contact details.

scenarios.label: Contradictory detail
scenarios.description: PDS data contradicts a local or route-specific fact and must be reviewed, not silently trusted.

scenarios.label: Partial field policy
scenarios.description: The response deliberately omits some fields to simulate minimum-necessary or partial-data posture.

scenarios.label: Throttled
scenarios.description: The adapter has crossed its safe retry budget and must fall back locally.

scenarios.label: Degraded
scenarios.description: Upstream PDS is unavailable or degraded; Service must continue safely without enrichment.

logging_policy.masking: All identifiers are masked to first 2 and last 2 characters only.
logging_policy.retention: In-memory only for rehearsal; no file persistence.

rollback_signals.threshold: more than 1 unresolved signal in the latest cohort or any confirmed wrong-patient event
more than 900ms across three consecutive monitoring windows
more than 5% contradictory-or-stale traces within the current cohort
Any route receives PDS rendering without an approved feature-flag tuple
